title: toolcall-repair-bench case
description: A case is one captured raw local-model output plus its auditable gold label. Each case records the exact string a model emitted, the tools that were offered in the request, a taxonomy of the malformed shape, and the single defensible gold tool call (or no_tool). Cases are frozen and hermetic so the corpus can be validated in CI.

id.description: Kebab-case slug identifying the case, e.g. "0001-json-in-content".

provenance.description: Where the raw output came from. Required so no case can be added without attribution.

kind.description: Whether the raw_output was captured verbatim from a live model run ("captured") or hand-authored to model a known failure shape ("synthetic"). This is the single authoritative provenance flag; the legacy boolean `synthetic` must agree with it.

model.description: The local model that produced the raw output (e.g. "gemma4:e4b-mlx"), or "synthetic" for hand-authored cases.

runtime.description: The inference runtime that served the model for a captured case (e.g. "ollama", "mlx", "llama.cpp"), or "unknown". Omit (or "n/a") for synthetic cases.

harness.description: The client/harness that issued the request for a captured case (e.g. "opencode", "openai-compatible", "codex-cli"), or "unknown". Omit (or "n/a") for synthetic cases.

prompt_id.description: Stable identifier for the prompt/task that elicited the output (e.g. "task-02-bugfix"), if known.

temperature.description: Decoding temperature for a captured case if known, else "unknown".

sampler.description: Sampler / decoding settings if known, else "unknown" (or "n/a" for synthetic).

captured.description: ISO date the output was captured or authored.

source.description: Where the raw output came from (repo, fixture path, capture session, or a description of the hand-authored shape).

synthetic.description: Legacy boolean mirror of `kind`. True iff kind == "synthetic". Retained for backward compatibility; `kind` is authoritative.

request_tools.description: The tools offered in the request, OpenAI-style function tools minus the outer wrapper. The scorer derives the list of valid tool NAMES from this array.

parameters.description: JSON Schema for the tool's parameters.

raw_output.description: The exact string the model emitted, reproduced byte-for-byte including trailing newlines.

taxonomy.description: One or more closed-enum labels describing the SHAPE of the malformed output.

gold.description: The single auditable gold label, defensible from raw_output alone.

name.description: Name of the gold tool call; must be one of the request_tools names.

arguments.description: The gold arguments object for the tool call.

note.description: Optional note explaining an ambiguous gold label. A note, not a guess.
